question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?

critical: Connectivity segment revenue grew 49.85% YoY.
Connectivity segment operating income grew 120.49% YoY.
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.23% YoY.
important: Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387 million in FY2025.
Connectivity segment revenue was $7,599 million in FY2024.
Connectivity segment operating income was $4,423 million in FY2025.
Connectivity segment operating income was $2,006 million in FY2024.
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168 million in FY2025.
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849 million in FY2024.
The historical financials in the S-1 have been retrospectively recast under common-control accounting.
The segment figures reflect the recast consolidated entity, not standalone SpaceX operations.
optional: FY 2024 connectivity impairment charge was $39 million.
Connectivity Impairment was $0 million in FY 2025.
X Holdings merged into xAI on March 28, 2025.

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing's subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.

critical: Connectivity Segment Revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 was $3,257 million.
Starlink had 10.3 million subscribers as of March 31, 2026.
Starlink Subscriber ARPU was $66 per month for Q1 2026.
Consumer revenue includes both recurring service fees and one-time Starlink Kit (terminal) hardware sales.
A Starlink Subscriber is defined as a unique Service Line assigned to a Starlink.com account that does not have a direct, negotiated agreement with the Starlink sales team.
The Starlink Subscribers metric excludes managed enterprise and government customers with negotiated contracts in aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and government entities.
Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109 million) is included in Connectivity revenue.
Enterprise and Government service revenue is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator.
ARPU is defined as recurring service revenue generated only from the self-service Starlink Subscriber base.
Product revenue is excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator.
important: Consumer Revenue was $2,148 million in Q1 2026.
Enterprise & Government Connectivity revenue was $1,109 million in Q1 2026.
Products Revenue from Starlink Kit sales was $380 million in Q1 2026.
Starlink had 8.9 million subscribers as of December 31, 2025.
Multiple people sharing one Service Line are counted as one subscriber.
The Starlink Subscribers metric counts unique Service Lines, not individual end users.
optional: SpaceX filed its S-1 on May 20, 2026.

question: Calculate AI and Connectivity segment capex intensity for FY2025 and Q1 2026. Compare quarter versus full-year intensity and assess what the change implies about the pace and stage of AI infrastructure buildout.

critical: AI Segment FY2025 Revenue was $3,201M
AI Segment Q1 2026 revenue was $818M
Connectivity Segment FY2025 revenue was $11,387M
Connectivity segment revenue was $3,257M in Q1 2026.
AI Segment FY2025 capital expenditures were $12,727M
AI segment capital expenditures were $7,723M in Q1 2026
Connectivity Segment FY2025 capital expenditures were $4,178M
Connectivity segment capital expenditures were $1,332M in Q1 2026.
important: The company expects a multi-year investment horizon before achieving sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA.
All figures were retrospectively recast under common-control accounting to include xAI and X Holdings.
optional: AI Segment revenue was $727M in Q1 2025.
AI Segment Q1 2025 capital expenditures were $2,567M.
The SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement was filed on May 20, 2026.
